name: atlassian-status-page
label: Atlassian Status Page
description: This connector enables automated operations such as creating incidents, retrieving incident lists, managing active maintenances, and more.

fields.title: Server URL
fields.name: server_url
fields.description: Specify the host or server URL to authenticate with the Atlassian Statuspage REST API endpoint.
fields.tooltip: Specify the host or server URL to authenticate with the Atlassian Statuspage REST API endpoint.

fields.title: API Key
fields.name: api_key
fields.description: Specify the API key to authenticate with the Atlassian Statuspage REST API endpoint.
fields.tooltip: Specify the API key to authenticate with the Atlassian Statuspage REST API endpoint.

fields.title: Verify SSL
fields.name: verify_ssl
fields.description: Specifies whether the SSL certificate for the server is to be verified. By default, this option is selected, i.e., set to true.
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the SSL certificate for the server is to be verified. By default, this option is selected, i.e., set to true.

operations.title: Create Incident
operations.description: Creates an incident in Atlassian Statuspage based on the provided input parameters.

parameters.name: page_id
parameters.title: Page ID
parameters.description: Specify the unique identifier of the status page from which the incident should be created.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the unique identifier of the status page from which the incident should be created.

parameters.name: name
parameters.title: Name
parameters.description: Specify the name of the incident to be shown on Atlassian Statuspage.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the name of the incident to be shown on Atlassian Statuspage.

parameters.title: Status
parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Select the status of the incident you want to create. Note: For realtime incidents, valid values are investigating, identified, monitoring, and resolved. For scheduled incidents, valid values are scheduled, in_progress, verifying, and completed
parameters.tooltip: Select the status of the incident you want to create. Note: For realtime incidents, valid values are investigating, identified, monitoring, and resolved. For scheduled incidents, valid values are scheduled, in_progress, verifying, and completed

Scheduled.name: scheduled_for
Scheduled.title: Scheduled from
Scheduled.description: Specifies the scheduled start time for the incident. This parameter is used when creating a scheduled incident (such as planned maintenance) rather than an active or real-time incident.
Scheduled.tooltip: Specifies the scheduled start time for the incident. This parameter is used when creating a scheduled incident (such as planned maintenance) rather than an active or real-time incident.

Scheduled.name: scheduled_until
Scheduled.title: Scheduled from
Scheduled.description: Specifies the scheduled end time for the incident. This parameter is used when creating a scheduled incident (typically planned maintenance) and defines when the maintenance is expected to be completed.
Scheduled.tooltip: Specifies the scheduled end time for the incident. This parameter is used when creating a scheduled incident (typically planned maintenance) and defines when the maintenance is expected to be completed.

Scheduled.title: Scheduled Remind Prior
Scheduled.name: scheduled_remind_prior
Scheduled.description: Specifies whether a reminder notification should be sent to subscribers before a scheduled incident begins.
Scheduled.tooltip: Specifies whether a reminder notification should be sent to subscribers before a scheduled incident begins.

Scheduled.title: Auto Transition to Maintenance State
Scheduled.name: auto_transition_to_maintenance_state
Scheduled.description: Specifies this parameter the incident will automatically change its status from scheduled to in_progress at the scheduled start time.
Scheduled.tooltip: Specifies this parameter the incident will automatically change its status from scheduled to in_progress at the scheduled start time.

Scheduled.title: Scheduled Auto In-Progress
Scheduled.name: scheduled_auto_in_progress
Scheduled.description: Determines whether the scheduled incident should automatically change its status to in_progress at the time specified in the scheduled_for field. Note: Atlassian may use scheduled_auto_in_progress in newer API versions or for backward compatibility with certain integrations, but auto_transition_to_maintenance_state is generally preferred in documentation.
Scheduled.tooltip: Specifies whether the incident should automatically transition to a resolved (operational) state at the time specified in the scheduled_until field. Note: Atlassian may use scheduled_auto_in_progress in newer API versions or for backward compatibility with certain integrations, but auto_transition_to_maintenance_state is generally preferred in documentation.

Scheduled.title: Auto Transition Deliver Notifications Start
Scheduled.name: auto_transition_deliver_notifications_at_start
Scheduled.description: Specifying this parameter notifications should be automatically sent to subscribers when a scheduled incident transitions to a resolved state at the scheduled end time (scheduled_until).
Scheduled.tooltip: Specifying this parameter notifications should be automatically sent to subscribers when a scheduled incident transitions to a resolved state at the scheduled end time (scheduled_until).

parameters.title: Impact Override
parameters.name: impact_override
parameters.description: Select the impact override for the incident.
parameters.tooltip: Select the impact override for the incident.

parameters.name: body
parameters.title: Comment
parameters.description: Specify the detailed description or comment message of the incident. Note: Maximum limit is 25000 characters.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the detailed description or comment message of the incident. Note: Maximum limit is 25000 characters.

parameters.name: deliver_notifications
parameters.title: Deliver Notifications
parameters.description: Deliver notifications to subscribers if this is true. If this is false, create an incident without notifying customers.
parameters.tooltip: Deliver notifications to subscribers if this is true. If this is false, create an incident without notifying customers.

parameters.name: components
parameters.title: Components
parameters.description: Map of status changes to apply to affected components.
parameters.tooltip: Map of status changes to apply to affected components.

parameters.name: component_ids
parameters.title: Component IDs
parameters.description: Specify a CSV list of of component_ids affected by this incident.
parameters.tooltip: Specify a CSV list of of component_ids affected by this incident.

parameters.name: metadata
parameters.title: Meta Data
parameters.description: Specify additional structured information(In JSON format)related information about the incident that doesn't belong to the main incident fields like name, status, or impact.
parameters.tooltip: Specify additional structured information(In JSON format)related information about the incident that doesn't belong to the main incident fields like name, status, or impact.

parameters.name: additional_fields
parameters.title: Additional Fields
parameters.description: Specify any additional parameters in JSON format to create an incident.
parameters.tooltip: Specify any additional parameters in JSON format to create an incident.

operations.title: Get Incident
operations.description: Retrieve a specific incident from from Atlassian Statuspage.

parameters.name: page_id
parameters.title: Page ID
parameters.description: Specify the unique identifier of the status page from which the incident should be deleted.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the unique identifier of the status page from which the incident should be deleted.

parameters.name: incident_id
parameters.title: Incident ID
parameters.description: Specify the unique identifier of the incident to be deleted.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the unique identifier of the incident to be deleted.

operations.title: Update Incident
operations.description: Updates the details of an existing incident on a specific status page. This can include modifying the incident’s name, status, impact, or other relevant fields.

parameters.name: page_id
parameters.title: Page ID
parameters.description: Specify the unique identifier of the status page from which the incident should be updated.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the unique identifier of the status page from which the incident should be updated.

parameters.name: incident_id
parameters.title: Incident ID
parameters.description: Specify the unique identifier of the incident to be updated.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the unique identifier of the incident to be updated.

parameters.name: name
parameters.title: Name
parameters.description: Specify the name of the incident to be updated.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the name of the incident to be updated.

parameters.title: Status
parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Select the status of the incident you want to update. Note: For realtime incidents, valid values are investigating, identified, monitoring, and resolved. For scheduled incidents, valid values are scheduled, in_progress, verifying, and completed
parameters.tooltip: Select the status of the incident you want to update. Note: For realtime incidents, valid values are investigating, identified, monitoring, and resolved. For scheduled incidents, valid values are scheduled, in_progress, verifying, and completed

Scheduled.name: scheduled_for
Scheduled.title: Scheduled from
Scheduled.description: Specifies the scheduled start time for the incident. This parameter is used when creating a scheduled incident (such as planned maintenance) rather than an active or real-time incident.
Scheduled.tooltip: Specifies the scheduled start time for the incident. This parameter is used when creating a scheduled incident (such as planned maintenance) rather than an active or real-time incident.

Scheduled.name: scheduled_until
Scheduled.title: Scheduled from
Scheduled.description: Specifies the scheduled end time for the incident. This parameter is used when creating a scheduled incident (typically planned maintenance) and defines when the maintenance is expected to be completed.
Scheduled.tooltip: Specifies the scheduled end time for the incident. This parameter is used when creating a scheduled incident (typically planned maintenance) and defines when the maintenance is expected to be completed.

Scheduled.title: Scheduled Remind Prior
Scheduled.name: scheduled_remind_prior
Scheduled.description: Specifies whether a reminder notification should be sent to subscribers before a scheduled incident begins.
Scheduled.tooltip: Specifies whether a reminder notification should be sent to subscribers before a scheduled incident begins.

Scheduled.title: Auto Transition to Maintenance State
Scheduled.name: auto_transition_to_maintenance_state
Scheduled.description: Specifies this parameter the incident will automatically change its status from scheduled to in_progress at the scheduled start time.
Scheduled.tooltip: Specifies this parameter the incident will automatically change its status from scheduled to in_progress at the scheduled start time.

Scheduled.title: Scheduled Auto In-Progress
Scheduled.name: scheduled_auto_in_progress
Scheduled.description: Determines whether the scheduled incident should automatically change its status to in_progress at the time specified in the scheduled_for field. Note: Atlassian may use scheduled_auto_in_progress in newer API versions or for backward compatibility with certain integrations, but auto_transition_to_maintenance_state is generally preferred in documentation.
Scheduled.tooltip: Specifies whether the incident should automatically transition to a resolved (operational) state at the time specified in the scheduled_until field. Note: Atlassian may use scheduled_auto_in_progress in newer API versions or for backward compatibility with certain integrations, but auto_transition_to_maintenance_state is generally preferred in documentation.

Scheduled.title: Auto Transition Deliver Notifications Start
Scheduled.name: auto_transition_deliver_notifications_at_start
Scheduled.description: Specifying this parameter notifications should be automatically sent to subscribers when a scheduled incident transitions to a resolved state at the scheduled end time (scheduled_until).
Scheduled.tooltip: Specifying this parameter notifications should be automatically sent to subscribers when a scheduled incident transitions to a resolved state at the scheduled end time (scheduled_until).

parameters.title: Impact Override
parameters.name: impact_override
parameters.description: Select the impact override for the incident.
parameters.tooltip: Select the impact override for the incident.

parameters.name: body
parameters.title: Comment
parameters.description: Specify the detailed description or comment message of the incident. Note: Maximum limit is 25000 characters.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the detailed description or comment message of the incident. Note: Maximum limit is 25000 characters.

parameters.name: deliver_notifications
parameters.title: Deliver Notifications
parameters.description: Deliver notifications to subscribers if this is true. If this is false, create an incident without notifying customers.
parameters.tooltip: Deliver notifications to subscribers if this is true. If this is false, create an incident without notifying customers.

parameters.name: components
parameters.title: Components
parameters.description: Map of status changes to apply to affected components.
parameters.tooltip: Map of status changes to apply to affected components.

parameters.name: component_ids
parameters.title: Component IDs
parameters.description: Specify a CSV list of of component ids affected by this incident.
parameters.tooltip: Specify a CSV list of of component ids affected by this incident.

parameters.name: metadata
parameters.title: Meta Data
parameters.description: Specify additional structured information(In JSON format)related information about the incident that doesn't belong to the main incident fields like name, status, or impact.
parameters.tooltip: specify additional structured information(In JSON format)related information about the incident that doesn't belong to the main incident fields like name, status, or impact.

parameters.name: additional_fields
parameters.title: Additional Fields
parameters.description: Specify any additional parameters in JSON format to create an incident.
parameters.tooltip: Specify any additional parameters in JSON format to create an incident.

operations.title: Delete Incident
operations.description: Deletes a specific incident from a status page. This operation permanently removes the incident and all its associated updates from the page.

parameters.name: page_id
parameters.title: Page ID
parameters.description: Specify the unique identifier of the status page from which the incident should be deleted.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the unique identifier of the status page from which the incident should be deleted.

parameters.name: incident_id
parameters.title: Incident ID
parameters.description: Specify the unique identifier of the incident to be deleted.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the unique identifier of the incident to be deleted.

operations.title: Get List Status Pages
operations.description: Retrieves a list of all status pages from Atlassian Statuspage.

operations.title: Get List Incidents
operations.description: Retrieves a list of active and historical incidents, including real-time outages and scheduled maintenance incidents associated with a specified Statuspage.

parameters.name: page_id
parameters.title: Page ID
parameters.description: Specify the unique identifier of the Statuspage from which you want to retrieve the list of incidents.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the unique identifier of the Statuspage from which you want to retrieve the list of incidents.

parameters.name: q
parameters.title: Query
parameters.description: If this is specified, search for the text query string in the incidents' name, status, postmortem_body, and incident_updates fields.
parameters.tooltip: If this is specified, search for the text query string in the incidents' name, status, postmortem_body, and incident_updates fields.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.title: Limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of rows to return per page. The default and maximum limit is 100.
parameters.tooltip: The maximum number of rows to return per page. The default and maximum limit is 100.

parameters.name: page
parameters.title: Offset
parameters.description: Specify the page offset for pagination.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the page offset for pagination.

operations.title: Get Active Maintenance Incidents
operations.description: Retrieves a list of all currently active scheduled maintenance incidents for the specified Statuspage.

parameters.name: page_id
parameters.title: Page ID
parameters.description: Specify the unique identifier of the status page for which you want to retrieve the list of active maintenance incidents.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the unique identifier of the status page for which you want to retrieve the list of active maintenance incidents.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.title: Limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of rows to return per page. The default and maximum limit is 100.
parameters.tooltip: The maximum number of rows to return per page. The default and maximum limit is 100.

parameters.name: page
parameters.title: Offset
parameters.description: Specify the page offset for pagination.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the page offset for pagination.

operations.title: Get Upcoming Incidents
operations.description: Retrieves a list of upcoming incidents scheduled for a specific status page. Upcoming incidents are those that have been created and scheduled but have not yet started.

parameters.name: page_id
parameters.title: Page ID
parameters.description: Specify the unique identifier of the status page for which you want to retrieve the list of active upcoming incidents.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the unique identifier of the status page for which you want to retrieve the list of active upcoming incidents.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.title: Limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of rows to return per page. The default and maximum limit is 100.
parameters.tooltip: The maximum number of rows to return per page. The default and maximum limit is 100.

parameters.name: page
parameters.title: Offset
parameters.description: Specify the page offset for pagination.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the page offset for pagination.

operations.title: Get Scheduled Incidents
operations.description: Retrieves a list of all scheduled incidents, including both upcoming and ongoing scheduled maintenance events, for a specific status page.

parameters.name: page_id
parameters.title: Page ID
parameters.description: Specify the unique identifier of the status page for which you want to retrieve the list of scheduled incidents.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the unique identifier of the status page for which you want to retrieve the list of scheduled incidents.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.title: Limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of rows to return per page. The default and maximum limit is 100.
parameters.tooltip: The maximum number of rows to return per page. The default and maximum limit is 100.

parameters.name: page
parameters.title: Offset
parameters.description: Specify the page offset for pagination.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the page offset for pagination.

operations.title: Get Unresolved Incidents
operations.description: Retrieves a list of all unresolved incidents for a specific status page. Unresolved incidents are those that are currently open and have not yet been marked as resolved

parameters.name: page_id
parameters.title: Page ID
parameters.description: Specify the unique identifier of the status page for which you want to retrieve the list of unresolved incidents.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the unique identifier of the status page for which you want to retrieve the list of unresolved incidents.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.title: Limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of rows to return per page. The default and maximum limit is 100.
parameters.tooltip: The maximum number of rows to return per page. The default and maximum limit is 100.

parameters.name: page
parameters.title: Offset
parameters.description: Specify the page offset for pagination.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the page offset for pagination.

operations.title: Execute an API Request
operations.description: Execute any Atlassian Status Page API endpoint using the specified API method, endpoint, and payload as input parameters.

parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.title: Endpoint
parameters.tooltip: Specify the REST API endpoint for the action to perform. Note: Do not include the host URL in the endpoint. ex /v1/pages/{page_id}/incidents/unresolved or /v1/pages/{page_id}/incidents/{incident_id}.
parameters.description: Specify the REST API endpoint for the action to perform. Note: Do not include the host URL in the endpoint. ex /v1/pages/{page_id}/incidents/unresolved or /v1/pages/{page_id}/incidents/{incident_id}.

parameters.name: method
parameters.title: Method
parameters.description: The HTTP Method to use

parameters.title: Query Parameters
parameters.name: query_params
parameters.description: Specify the he Rest API query parameters object in JSON format.

parameters.title: Request Body/Payload
parameters.name: payload
parameters.description: The payload needed for the call. Use empty brackets if there is no payload.
